audit_items.proof_trace: ldap_rename dereferences msgidp at L151 and passes dn/newrdn to ber_printf without NULL checks, unlike compare.c/add.c/delete.c which have explicit assert guards; however, modify.c follows the same missing-assert pattern, confirming this is a library-wide API-contract convention, not a modrdn.c-specific defect. All callers within modrdn.c itself (ldap_rename2, ldap_rename_s) pass stack-address arguments, so no in-file NPD path exists. As a client library, ldap_rename is invoked by application code sending requests to an LDAP server; no external network path allows an attacker to supply NULL arguments to these functions, making the reachability claim inapplicable.
